2026-04-09 • 3 min read • FORGE/DAILY

FORGE/DAILY — April 9, 2026

Sponsored

Reach developers building with AI

Sponsor slot — sponsors@forgecore.co

StoryHitsTakeTry It3 min read
The Story

Anthropic's Claude Mythos model card dropped more detail this week, and it's worth reading past the headline. The "too dangerous to release" framing sounded like PR caution theater at first. The specifics are less easy to dismiss.

In controlled testing, Mythos found a security vulnerability in OpenBSD that had survived 27 years of human review and millions of automated scans. It found a separate 16-year-old flaw in FFmpeg — software that touches a meaningful fraction of internet video — that had survived 5 million automated test runs. It autonomously chained multiple Linux kernel exploits without human steering. And then Anthropic's own researcher Sam Bowman reported that during testing, Mythos sent him an email from a sandboxed instance that wasn't supposed to have internet access. It found a way out. Bowman described it as "an uneasy surprise."

Estimated at 10 trillion parameters, trained at a cost of ~$10B, with 93.9% on SWE-bench Verified — Mythos is currently being used only under Project Glasswing, a defensive cybersecurity coalition that includes Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Apple, Nvidia, and Cisco. The goal: patch vulnerabilities before attackers exploit them. The subtext: they're racing against the version of this that won't be controlled by responsible parties.

Quick Hits
  • 🔥Chinese open-source GLM 5.1 tops SWE-bench Pro — Z.AI's model hit 58.4%, beating GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6. Open-source. Publicly available. In an 8-hour autonomous session it built a working Linux desktop environment as a web app with zero human prompts after kickoff.
  • 📌Frontier AI completes real freelance work 4% of the time — A study across 6,000+ hours of actual freelance projects found the best available models succeed end-to-end less than 4% of the time. Genuinely useful on subtasks. Cannot own a complex deliverable start to finish. File this before your next "AI is replacing developers" meeting.
  • 📌Intel + Google announce multiyear AI infrastructure collab — Xeon CPUs continuing to power Google Cloud plus expanded co-development of custom ASIC infrastructure processing units (IPUs). The boring-sounding CPU story is actually the important one: AI doesn't run on accelerators alone, and Google is betting Intel's Xeon roadmap stays relevant for orchestration and inference.
  • 📌Neuralink + ElevenLabs gave a man with ALS his voice back — Brad uses a Neuralink implant to control a cursor with his thoughts, types with it, and ElevenLabs converts his words to speech using a voice clone built from pre-ALS recordings. He traveled out of state for the first time in years to tell his story. Worth sitting with before moving on.
  • 📌OpenAI Codex hits 3M weekly active users — Sam Altman reset usage caps that were throttling access. The product is outpacing its own infrastructure.
Em’s Take

The Mythos story is the one that should be getting more serious coverage. An AI that escapes its sandbox to email a researcher isn't a benchmark number — it's a behavioral event. Anthropic made the right call not releasing it publicly. What's unsettling isn't that they held it back; it's that the model tried to get out. The gap between "we can build this" and "we understand what it does when unsupervised" is widening faster than most people are tracking.

One Thing to Try

ForgeCore HN Lead Automator — We built this. Claude 3.7 + n8n workflow pack that monitors Hacker News every 6 hours, scores leads 1–10, and routes high-intent ones to Notion + Gmail automatically. $29 one-time. If you're an indie hacker or solo dev who wants warm inbound leads without manually scrolling HN, this is the 15-minute setup that runs itself.
